Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n    //\n    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n\n    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\", \"_miroir_metadata\": {\"key\": \"val\"}});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_custom\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_metadata\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n    ///\n    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n        struct TestCase {\n            doc: Value,\n            description: &'static str,\n            has_shard: bool,\n            has_updated_at: bool,\n            has_expires_at: bool,\n        }\n\n        let test_cases = vec![\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_expires_at\": \"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_expires_at allowed when ttl.disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: true,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\"}),\n                description: \"non-reserved _miroir_ fields allowed\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        for tc in test_cases {\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_updated_at`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify all required fields are present\n        assert!(!err.message.is_empty());\n        assert!(!err.code.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert!(err.link.is_some());\n        assert!(err.link.unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_orchestrator_shard_injection_flow() {\n        // Simulate the orchestrator injection flow:\n        // 1. Client sends document WITHOUT _miroir_shard\n        // 2. Validation passes (no _miroir_shard present)\n        // 3. Orchestrator injects _miroir_shard for routing\n        // 4. Write proceeds with injected field\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n\n        // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation at line 279-290)\n        let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(5);\n\n        assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], 5, \"orchestrator should inject _miroir_shard\");\n        assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be present\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_validation_order() {\n        // _miroir_shard is checked BEFORE primary key validation (per acceptance criteria)\n        // This ensures clients can't bypass validation by including both fields\n\n        let doc_with_shard_no_pk = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n        // The validation should catch _miroir_shard first, not missing primary key\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    }\n\n    // P2.9: Complete reserved field matrix tests\n    //\n    // Matrix cells per plan §5:\n    // | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    // |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    // | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED        | REJECTED       |\n    // | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (AE)  |\n    // | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (TTL) |\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard: Always reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn   }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n258\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n259\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n260\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n264\t// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n265\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n266\t\n267\t#[tokio::test]\n268\tasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n269\t    let shards = 3u32;\n270\t    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n271\t\n272\t    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n273\t    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n274\t\n275\t    // Build covering set for page 1\n276\t    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n277\t\n278\t    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n279\t        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n280\t        for \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n285\t            }));\n286\t        }\n287\t        let response = json!({\n288\t            \"hits\": hits,\n289\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n290\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n291\t        });\n292\t        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n293\t        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n294\t            total_docs: 50,\n295\t            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n296\t            term_stats: set)\n320\t    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n321\t    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n322\t    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n323\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n324\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n325\t        offset: 5,\n326\t        limit: 5,\n327\t        filter: None,\n328\t        facets: None,\n329\t        ranking_score: true,\n330\t        body: json!({}),\n331\t        global_idf: None,\n332\t    };\n333\t    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n334\t        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n335\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n336\t    ).await.unwrap();\n337\t\n338\t    // Pages should not overlap\n339\t    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n340\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n341\t        .collect();\n342\t    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n343\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n344\t        .collect();\n345\t\n346\t    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n347\t    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n348\t\n349\t    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n350\t    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n351\t    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n352\t\n353\t    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n354\t    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n355\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n356\t        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n357\t    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n358\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n359\t        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n360\t    assert!(\n361\t        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n362\t        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n363\t        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n364\t    );\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n368\t// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n369\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n370\t\n371\t#[test]\n372\tfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n373\t    let shards = 4u32;\n374\t    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n375\t\n376\t    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n377\t    for i in 2..4u32 {\n378\t        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n379\t            .unwrap()\n380\t            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n381\t            .unwrap();\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n385\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n386\t    for group in topo.groups() {\n387\t        if group.id == 1 {\n388\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n389\t            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n394\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n395\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n396\t        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n397\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n398\t                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n399\t            })\n400\t            .collect();\n401\t        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[test]\n406\tfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n407\t    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n408\t    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n409\t    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n410\t    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n411\t    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n412\t\n413\t    let rf = 2usize;\n414\t    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n415\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n416\t\n417\t    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n418\t    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n419\t    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n420\t\n421\t    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n422\t    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n423\t    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n424\t\n425\t    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n426\t    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n427\t        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n428\t    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n429\t\n430\t    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n431\t    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n432\t    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n436\t// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n437\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n438\t\n439\t#[test]\n440\tfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n441\t    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n442\t    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n443\t    for code in ALL_CODES {\n444\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n445\t        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n446\t        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n447\t\n448\t        // Must have all four fields\n449\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n450\t Phase 2 DoD integration tests.\n//!\n//! Tests covering all Definition of Done criteria:\n//! - 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n//! - Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n//! - Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n//! - Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n//! - Write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - Error-format parity: every error matches Meilisearch shape\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches plan §10 shape\n\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{ErrorType, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    MockNodeClient, SearchRequest,\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter,\n};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 3-node, 1 replica group topology\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn three_node_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 3);\n    for i in 0..3u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology (4 nodes)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 2);\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            1,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// All MiroirCode variants for iteration\nconst ALL_CODES: [MiroirCode; 10] = [\n    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n    MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n    MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n    MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n    MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n    MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n    MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n    MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 1: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&pk, shards);\n        shard_counts.entry(shard).or_default().push(pk);\n    }\n\n    // Every shard should have at least one document\n    assert_eq!(shard_counts.len(), shards as usize, \"all shards should receive documents\");\n\n    // All write targets should be reachable\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 3, \"RF=3 means 3 write targets per shard\");\n        for node_id in &targets {\n            assert!(topo.node(node_id).is_some(), \"target node {} should exist\", node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Total count should be 1000\n    let total: usize = shard_counts.values().map(|v| v.len()).sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 2: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_deduplication() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Compute covering set for query_seq=0\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, shards);\n\n    // Build per-node responses by accumulating all docs for each node.\n    // Multiple shards may map to the same node; a real Meilisearch node\n    // returns all its matching docs in a single response.\n    let mut node_hits: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut node_totals: HashMap<NodeId, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let doc1 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-a\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n        });\n        let doc2 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-b\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2 + 1),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n        });\n\n        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n        hits.push(doc1);\n        hits.push(doc2);\n        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 100,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &mock,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for hit in &result.hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n    }\n\n    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n    // the aggregation contract.\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n\n    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n\n    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n            for (value, count) in values {\n                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let shards = 3u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Build covering set for page 1\n    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..5u32 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n            }));\n        }\n        let response = json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n        });\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 5,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n\n    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n\n    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n    assert!(\n        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n\n    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n    for code in ALL_CODES {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n\n        // Must have all four fields\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n\n        // Code must start with miroir_\n        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n  let type_str = parsed[\"type\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            [\"invalid_request\", \"auth\", \"internal\", \"system\"].contains(&type_str),\n            \"{:?}: type '{}' is not a valid Meilisearch error type\",\n            code, type_str\n        );\n\n        // Link must be a string pointing to docs\n        let link_str = parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            link_str.contains(\"docs/errors.md\"),\n            \"{:?}: link '{}' should point to error docs\",\n            code, link_str\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_meilisearch_error_preserves_shape() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#index_not_found\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meili_body).unwrap();\n    let roundtrip = }\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t#[test]\n425\tfn test_shard_distribution_evenness() {\n426\t    let shard_count = 8u32;\n427\t    let doc_count = 1000;\n428\t\n429\t    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n430\t\n431\t    for i in 0..doc_count {\n432\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n433\t        let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n434\t
